data.body:
PASS 1 review (G6 — approach soundness): CLEAN, no findings.

Affirmations:
- Mechanism correctness: Docs-only deliverable; data flow (identify 4 reuse subsystems → document signatures/return shapes/invariants → verify against source) is complete. The drift/staleness failure case is explicitly handled by AC: "Each documented signature/return shape is verified against the current source (no drift)." No ordering/atomicity/concurrency concerns apply.
- Fitness-for-purpose: Solves the named problem (no single developer-facing API REFERENCE for the reuse surface) rather than a proxy, and explicitly distinguishes itself from the existing FRAMEWORK/USAGE/EVENT-level docs (llm-framework.md, event-schema.md, workflow-authoring-v2.md).
- Approach selection: Reviewer-generated alternatives — (a) autodoc/docstring generation, rejected because the plan needs invariants, custody model, verdict vocabulary, and worked reuse examples autodoc can't express; (b) folding into existing docs, which the plan already permits ("or focused additions to existing docs"). Chosen approach (dedicated reuse reference, cross-linked) is defensibly at-least-as-good; no clearly-superior missed alternative.
- Positive rationale present: the "Why" section states why this fits ("no single developer-facing API REFERENCE describing the reuse surface itself … for the next agent that needs to build ON this machinery").

Grounding note: all named symbols across the four targets resolve to real source — sign_manifest/verify_signature (src/rebar/signing.py), get_runner/RunRequest (src/rebar/llm/runner.py), run_workflow (src/rebar/llm/workflow/executor.py), resolve_prompt (src/rebar/llm/prompts.py), validate_structured (src/rebar/llm/findings.py). The documentation targets are not phantom APIs.
